notes: All traits are reusable across any canid. Values are normalized 0.0–1.0 unless otherwise noted. 'dominant' and 'recessive' fields reflect Mendelian tendency; complex polygenic traits use 'polygenic: true'.

morphology.name: Body Size
morphology.notes: Master scalar. Influences mass, height, stamina cost, and litter size. Polygenic — offspring tend toward midpoint with variance.

morphology.name: Leg Length Ratio
morphology.notes: Relative to body length. Affects terrain traversal speed and visual silhouette.

morphology.name: Skull Robustness
morphology.notes: Higher values = broader, heavier skull. Correlates to bite force. Borophagus reference = 1.0.

morphology.name: Muzzle Length
morphology.notes: 0.1 = extremely brachycephalic, 1.0 = hyper-elongated (maned wolf). Crosses of extremes produce intermediate muzzles.

morphology.name: Ear Type
morphology.notes: Ear carriage affects trainability perception and auditory predator/prey detection stat.

morphology.name: Tail Type
morphology.notes: Cosmetic plus social signal stat modifier.

morphology.name: Body Mass Modifier
morphology.notes: Multiplier on species baseline mass. 1.0 = average. Affects endurance, thermoregulation, fertility.

coat.name: Coat Length
coat.notes: Short coat dominant over long in most crosses. Ultra-dense is a double-layer modifier.

coat.name: Base Coat Color
coat.notes: Maps to a color shader parameter. Agouti (wolf-gray banding) is ancestral dominant.

coat.name: Coat Pattern
coat.notes: Merle is a modifier, not base pattern. Painted pattern specific to Lycaon — exotic in crosses. Brindle is partially dominant.

coat.name: Seasonal Coat Dimorphism
coat.notes: Present in arctic fox and some wolf subspecies. Dominant in crosses. Enables winter/summer coat switching.

coat.name: Coat Texture
coat.notes: Affects visual roughness parameter in 3D shader.

behavior.name: Temperament (Boldness)
behavior.notes: 0.0 = extremely fearful/aggressive, 1.0 = highly trusting/social. Strongly influenced by trainabilityBaseline. Domestic dogs skew high.

behavior.name: Prey Drive
behavior.notes: High prey drive is semi-dominant in crosses. Wolf/wild dog pairings push offspring toward higher values.

behavior.name: Pack Bonding Tendency
behavior.notes: Affects social behavior mechanics. High in gray wolf, African wild dog. Low in red fox, maned wolf.

behavior.name: Trainability
behavior.notes: Correlates with domestication history. Synergistic with temperament — high both = highly trainable hybrids.

behavior.name: Vocalization Type
behavior.notes: Affects audio flavor. Dhole = whistle_chirp; canids without true bark (wolves, foxes) inherit bark if crossed with domestic dog.

ecology.name: Climate Tolerance
ecology.notes: Offspring inherit the union of parental tolerances, biased toward the more tolerant parent. Arctic coat genes may unlock arctic tolerance.

ecology.name: Diet Specialization
ecology.notes: Crosses tend to drift toward omnivore unless both parents are hypercarnivore.

ecology.name: Habitat Preference
ecology.notes: Wide hybrid may require habitat enrichment to remain healthy in kennel.

health.name: Immune System Vigor
health.notes: Hybrid vigor (heterosis) can boost this above either parent. Inbreeding depresses it.

health.name: Lifespan Potential
health.notes: Normalized across canid range (~3–20 years). Small canids live longer in wild; domestic dogs inverted.

health.name: Inbreeding Coefficient
health.notes: Computed dynamically. Values above 0.25 begin applying health penalties. Above 0.50 = severe penalties.

health.name: Genetic Stability
health.notes: Decreases with cross-genus/cross-tribe pairings. Below 0.3 = high mutation chance. Extinct + extant pairings start at 0.4 max.

fertility.name: Fertility Rate
fertility.notes: Base fertility from species. Penalized by inbreeding, genetic instability, cross-genus pairing.

fertility.name: Litter Size Potential
fertility.notes: Larger body mass = larger litter potential. [SCIENTIFIC reference: Smith et al. 2011]

fertility.name: Sterility Risk
fertility.notes: Increases sharply in cross-tribe or cross-subfamily pairings. Sandbox only for most distant pairings.

genetic_meta.name: Mutation Rate Modifier
genetic_meta.notes: Derived from genetic_stability. Low stability = higher chance of random trait mutation each generation.

genetic_meta.name: DNA Completeness (Extinct Only)
genetic_meta.notes: Represents how complete the reconstructed genome is for extinct species. 0.0 = placeholder only. Affects fertility and stability of offspring involving extinct parents.

morph_targets_3d.description: Uniform XYZ scale of full body mesh
Y-axis scale on leg bones only
X-axis scale on skull mesh region
Z-axis forward extension of muzzle mesh
Blend shape between floppy and full erect ear
Blend shape from thin straight to bushy curl
Shader parameter for fur strand length
RGB tint applied to base albedo texture
Lerp between summer and winter coat texture sets
PBR roughness value for coat material
